strengths: Nearly 20 years of hands-on software engineering across web, embedded, low-latency, and infrastructure systems
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform running on Kubernetes
Recent hands-on AI-agent work: MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks at re:cinq
concerns: No director/principal-level scope — career history is senior/staff IC roles without organization-wide technical leadership
No recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products/platforms; AI exposure is recent (2025) and tooling-level
No Master's/PhD in an AI-related field, and limited evidence of cloud-strategy or customer-advisory experience

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and AI-built products
Seniority gap versus director-level strategic scope
Meets minimum bar: BS in CS plus ~15+ years engineering experience

response.justification: Against the role's core competencies: the 15-year experience minimum is met (professional work since 2006, BS in Computer Science), and entrepreneurial experience is evidenced by founding GISCollective. However, the role centers on recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI products — the résumé shows only recent MCP/agentic tooling contributions at re:cinq, with no shipped AI product or platform cited. Cloud fluency is present but tactical (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployment) rather than strategic. There is no evidence of advising strategic customers, defining product future-state, or influencing at the director level; conference talks (FOSS4G) and workshops show knowledge-sharing but not industry-recognized AI thought leadership. The evidence supports a strong senior/staff engineer, not a Principal Engineer for AI strategy.
